title: Against Method
excerpt: A devastating anarchistic attack on the idea that science follows any single rational method — arguing that the only rule that survives historical scrutiny is "anything goes."
keyIdeas: Epistemological anarchism: science has no fixed method
Anything goes — the only principle that history supports
Counterinduction as a legitimate scientific strategy
Incommensurability between rival paradigms and theories
Galileo succeeded through propaganda, not rational argument
Science must be separated from the state
keyTakeaways: No universal methodological rule has been followed in the history of science — every rule has been violated with profit
Scientific progress often requires developing theories that contradict established facts
The authority of science is not rational but institutional — science is one tradition among many
A free society must separate science from the state, as it does church and state
whoShouldRead: Scientists troubled by the gap between textbook method and actual practice
Critics of scientific authority and scientism
Anyone who suspects that scientific progress is messier than the official story admits
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking a systematic philosophical treatise
Those who believe science is a purely rational, rule-governed enterprise
People offended by polemical style, irony, and deliberate provocation
